title: ABCA Workflow
description: A versioned, declarative description of how the agent executes one kind of task. See docs/design/WORKFLOWS.md and ADR-014. Phase-0 schema for issue #248.

version.description: Semantic version, immutable per published version. Major must match the id's -vN suffix.

domain.description: Drives admission defaults and eval/cost segmentation.

description.description: One-line natural-language summary, read by humans and by an agent selecting a workflow. Powers registry search / 'bgagent workflow list' (#246). Distinct from the machine-facing prompt.

guidance.description: Optional longer 'how to use this workflow' note (patterns, examples, constraints) surfaced at discovery time, not injected into the agent prompt.

requires_repo.description: Whether a GitHub clone / PR finalization is mandatory. Defaults from domain (coding=true, knowledge=false) when omitted.

read_only.description: When true, the agent may not mutate the working tree: selects the read-only Cedar principal and excludes Write/Edit from allowed_tools.

template.description: Inline system-prompt fragment, or a registry reference (registry://prompt/<id>).

placeholders.description: Placeholder names the base template will substitute (e.g. repo_url, task_id, memory_context).

sources.description: Context sources the orchestrator assembles. Repo-less workflows must exclude 'issue'/'pull_request'.

agent_config.description: Everything that shapes the Claude Agent SDK session for this task type. Asset kinds mirror the #246 registry vocabulary (capability/skill/plugin/mcp_server/prompt_fragment/cedar_policy_module). Layered over repo-discovered config (.claude/, .mcp.json) per repo_config below.

tier.description: Maps to the SECURITY.md tool tier. Governs how permissive the rest of agent_config may be.

model.description: Optional preferred Bedrock model for this task type. A suggestion, not a guarantee: the per-task max_budget and the repo Blueprint / platform allow-list still bound it (see WORKFLOWS.md override precedence). Omit to inherit the Blueprint/platform default.

id.description: Bedrock model id, e.g. anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 / a cross-region inference profile. Validated against the platform/Blueprint allow-list at resolution; an unpermitted id fails admission rather than silently downgrading.

allow_task_override.description: Whether a per-task model override (if the API exposes one) may supersede this. Default true.

allowed_tools.description: Built-in tools exposed to the agent SDK (Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, WebFetch). Cedar still gates per-call. read_only workflows must exclude Write/Edit. Replaces the hardcoded list in runner.py.

mcp_servers.description: MCP server references. builtin/<name> for shipped servers (e.g. builtin/linear); registry://mcp/<id> once #246 lands. SHIPPED in Phase 1-3 only for builtin refs.

cedar_policy_modules.description: Cedar policy module references (builtin/<name> or registry://cedar/<id>). builtin/hard_deny is always-on; builtin/soft_deny is mandatory when read_only:false (policy floor).

skills.description: REGISTRY (Phase 4, #246). Agent skill packages (prompt + tool guidance) to load for this task type, by ref (registry://skill/<id>). Ignored by the runner until #246 lands.

plugins.description: REGISTRY (Phase 4, #246). Claude Code / agent plugin bundles, by ref (registry://plugin/<id>). Ignored by the runner until #246 lands.

subagents.description: REGISTRY (Phase 4, #246). Subagent definitions (analogous to .claude/agents/*.md) the agent may delegate to, by ref. Does NOT lift the single run_agent invariant (#99) — these are SDK-internal delegations, not additional top-level agent loops. Ignored until #246 lands.

prompt_fragments.description: REGISTRY (Phase 4, #246). Reusable rule / system-prompt fragments (analogous to .claude/rules/*.md) appended to the workflow prompt, by ref (registry://prompt/<id>). Ignored until #246 lands.

repo_config.description: How this workflow relates to a source-control repository: which VCS provider, and how config discovered from the cloned repo (CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/, .claude/agents/, .claude/settings.json, .mcp.json) is layered. Provider is an abstraction so non-GitHub backends can be added later without a schema break; 'github' is the only implemented provider today.

provider.description: VCS provider abstraction. 'github' is the only implemented backend today; others are reserved so workflows declare intent provider-neutrally. The platform maps the provider to its clone, auth (token secret), and change-proposal (PR/MR) semantics — see the VcsProvider interface in WORKFLOWS.md. Forbidden when requires_repo:false.

discover.description: Whether to load repo-discovered config via setting_sources=['project']. Must be false (or omitted) when requires_repo:false (no repo to discover from).

ignore.description: Specific repo config sources this workflow opts out of even when discover:true (e.g. a locked-down workflow ignoring repo .mcp.json or .claude/settings.json).

required_inputs.description: Submit-time validation contract. Replaces scattered per-task-type required-input checks.

steps.description: Ordered pipeline phases the agent-side step runner interprets.

kind.description: Dispatches to a registered step handler.

name.description: Optional human-readable step label, used in progress milestones and error attribution.

on_failure.description: Runner behavior when this step fails.

gate.description: For verify_build/verify_lint: how the result gates success.

target.description: For deliver_artifact: the name of a registered Python deliverer (workflow/deliverers.py / DELIVERERS) to route the produced output through. Open string, not a closed enum, so a new delivery method is a registered deliverer, not a schema change (ADR-014 addendum 2026-06-08). First-party names: s3, comment, s3_and_comment. Each deliverer declares the terminal outcomes it produces; validator rule 11 consults that registry.

post_hooks.description: RESERVED. Post-agent gates are expressed as steps (verify_build, ensure_pr, deliver_artifact, ...); this field is retained only because issue #248 names it, and is not interpreted by the runner. Do not use — author gates as steps to avoid dual-expression ambiguity.

terminal_outcomes.description: What 'done' produces; drives finalization success inference.

limits.description: Workflow-level defaults; per-task and per-repo (Blueprint) still override per the override-precedence rules.

promotion_gate.description: The check contract a version must pass to reach 'production'. Promotion is earned, not set: status:production is rejected until these checks pass. Optional until the eval harness (#236) lands; when absent, promotion falls back to the unit/handler test tier as a reviewed manual step. See docs/design/WORKFLOWS.md#promotion-is-earned-not-set.

requires.description: Check ids that must pass before validated->production. Pre-#236: a concrete test target such as 'tests:agent/new_task'. Post-#236: an eval id such as 'eval:web-research-quality'.

status.description: Promotion lifecycle state. Only 'production' resolves for normal tasks. The validated->production transition requires promotion_gate.requires checks to pass.
